name: Tour of Ibiza
name_original: Vuelta a la Isla de Eivissa/Ibiza
description: This difficult 77.8-mile road cycling tour of Ibiza offers diverse terrain, 5772 feet of elevation gain, and stunning coastal and inland sce
region_intro: Road cycling around Ibiza offers diverse terrain, from coastal roads with Mediterranean views to inland routes through pine forests and rugged hills. The island features rolling roads, short, steep ascents, and routes with significant elevation gain. Its landscape provides a mix of well-paved surfaces and more challenging sections. Cyclists can find routes suitable for various skill levels across the island. Best road cycling routes around Ibiza The most popular road cycling route is Tour of Ibiza, a 77.8 miles (125.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the island's varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is West coast of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany, a difficult 44.8 miles (72.1 km) path. This route offers coastal views and challenging sections. Local road cyclists also love the Cala Portinatx, Cala Roig, Punta Grossa and Aigües Blanques, a 54.1 miles (87.1 km) trail leading through the northern, hillier areas, often completed in about 4 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Ibiza is defined by coastal roads, pine forests, and rugged hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes to suit different ability levels. The routes in Ibiza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 8,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ibiza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: Ibiza offers a diverse landscape for road cyclists. You'll find picturesque coastal roads with breathtaking Mediterranean views, tranquil inland routes winding through fragrant pine forests, and rugged hills that provide both challenging climbs and panoramic vistas. The island features rolling roads, short, steep ascents, and routes with significant elevation gain, catering to various preferences.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Ibiza for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Ibiza has several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000752479/tour-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 77.8-mile (125.3 km) trail exploring the island's varied landscapes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000780839/west-coast-of-ibiza-circular-from-sant-antoni-de-portmany" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West coast of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 44.8-mile (72.1 km) path known for its coastal views and challenging sections. You can also find significant climbs in areas like Serra Grossa.

Q: What are some easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ibiza?
A: For those seeking a more relaxed ride, Ibiza offers easier options. A great choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000271212/from-sant-francesc-de-formentera-to-faro-de-barbaria-return-via-the-ses-salines-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sant Francesc de Formentera to Faro de Barbaria — return via the Ses Salines Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 20.9-mile (33.7 km) route on the nearby island of Formentera, which is known for its flat terrain. In total, there are nearly 60 easy routes available for road cycling in Ibiza.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Ibiza?
A: The Mediterranean climate ensures warm weather year-round, making spring and autumn particularly prime months for cycling. During these seasons, the temperatures are pleasant, and the island is less crowded. Summer riding is also possible, especially in early mornings or late afternoons, to avoid the intense heat.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Ibiza are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000780839/west-coast-of-ibiza-circular-from-sant-antoni-de-portmany" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West coast of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001603143/roads-in-the-center-of-the-island-of-ibiza-circular-from-sant-antoni-de-portmany-to-santa-gertrudis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roads in the center of the island of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany to Santa Gertrudis&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: Ibiza's routes often showcase stunning natural beauty and cultural sites. You might pass by coastal features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/334642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala d'en Serra&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Portinatx&lt;/a&gt;. Inland, you can explore areas with pine forests and hills. Cultural landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/453253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary of the Snows&lt;/a&gt; in Ibiza Town, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5301455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Arabí Hippy Market&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on a route from Santa Eulària des Riu.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: While many routes are designed for more experienced cyclists, there are options suitable for families, especially those with older children or those comfortable with moderate distances. The nearby island of Formentera, accessible by ferry, is particularly known for its flat, quiet roads, making it a great choice for family cycling. Look for routes marked as 'easy' or 'moderate' for suitable options.

Q: How do other road cyclists rate the routes in Ibiza?
A: The road cycling routes in Ibiza are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 8,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the island's diverse terrain, often praising the scenic coastal views and the variety of challenging and moderate paths available.

Q: Are there routes that explore the northern, hillier parts of Ibiza?
A: Yes, the northern regions of Ibiza are known for their greener, hillier terrain. A route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001477289/cala-portinatx-cala-roig-punta-grossa-and-aiguees-blanques" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Portinatx, Cala Roig, Punta Grossa and Aigües Blanques&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.1-mile (87.1 km) trail, leads through these areas, offering significant elevation and rewarding views.

Q: What is the total number of road cycling routes available in Ibiza?
A: There are over 300 road cycling routes available in Ibiza, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. This includes nearly 60 easy routes, over 220 moderate routes, and close to 30 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the center of the island?
A: Yes, you can explore the island's interior. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001603143/roads-in-the-center-of-the-island-of-ibiza-circular-from-sant-antoni-de-portmany-to-santa-gertrudis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roads in the center of the island of Ibiza — circular from Sant Antoni de Portmany to Santa Gertrudis&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.4-mile (55.4 km) route that takes you through the island's central areas, offering a different perspective away from the coast.

Q: Can I cycle to the Serra Grossa mountain in Ibiza?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenging climb, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001484819/sant-antoni-de-portmany-castillo-de-ibiza-return-via-serra-grossa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Antoni de Portmany - Castillo de Ibiza — return via Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt; incorporate sections around Serra Grossa, which is one of Ibiza's highest mountains, providing demanding ascents and panoramic views.
tips: winding road along the coast, great views, excellent surface

Nice 7 km climb, soft at the start and the a relatively steady 5-7%. Better ride it in the spring or the fall to avoud the summer traffic and heat. From the top you can continue exploring the north of Ibiza, or plange down to Porrinatx or Cala Xarraca for a swim
Fields of almond trees that are close to the town of Sant Mateu d'Albarca.

Leave the infamous San Antonio behind you as you climb up and out along the PM-812. The climb swings up through the woods and delivers you above the tree line. Appreciate the smooth road surface, short flat section and epic views back towards the sea before the road kicks up again. Keep pushing on but save a little in reserve as the hill has one final bit of up following the brief downhill rest bite.

For many, this cove is one of the most beautiful on the island. It is famous and usually crowded.
If the weather is good and you are not in a hurry, I recommend that you get off your bike and soak your feet for a while enjoying this very special place.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza?
faqs.answer: This is considered a difficult road cycling tour, requiring very good fitness. It covers approximately 125 kilometers with significant elevation gains of around 1,760 meters. While the island has rolling terrain, the full tour includes substantial ascents, making it a demanding ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The tour offers constantly changing and picturesque scenery. You'll pass through spectacular inland landscapes, dense pine forests, and magnificent coastal settings with breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea. Expect golden sandy beaches, charming coastal villages, and rural landscapes with traditional Ibicenco houses. Notable sights include the Almond Fields Near Sant Mateu d'Albarca and various coastal vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza?
faqs.answer: Ibiza's mild year-round climate makes it an attractive cycling destination outside the peak summer months. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and less crowded roads. Even winter can be suitable for cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging climbs or sections to be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, the tour includes several notable climbs. Be prepared for the ascent from Cala Sant Vicent to Sant Joan de Labritja, which is about 6 kilometers long with sections reaching up to 12% gradient. Another challenging part is the climb along the PM-812 road from Sant Antoni towards Sant Agnès, known for its smooth surface and epic views. The Sa Serra Grossa area also presents demanding routes.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza. The roads are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling on public roads, dogs are generally not recommended for this type of long-distance, difficult road cycling tour due to safety concerns for both the cyclist and the animal. If you are looking for dog-friendly activities, consider shorter, less trafficked routes or walking trails where dogs can be safely leashed.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route around the island, you can typically start and park in any of the main towns or villages along the coast, such as Ibiza Town, Sant Antoni de Portmany, or Santa Eulària des Riu. Public parking is available in these areas, though it may be paid or limited during peak season. There isn't one single designated 'trailhead' for the entire island tour.

faqs.question: Are there any unpaved sections on the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza?
faqs.answer: While approximately 80% of the route is paved and suitable for road bikes, some segments, particularly in more remote areas, may be unpaved. Road cyclists should be aware of this possibility and exercise caution on such sections.

faqs.question: Can I rent a road bike in Ibiza for this tour?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ibiza has several bike rental shops, especially in larger towns like Ibiza Town, Sant Antoni, and Santa Eulària. Many offer high-quality road bikes suitable for a tour of this nature. It's advisable to book in advance, especially during busier seasons.

faqs.question: What should I pack for cycling the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza?
faqs.answer: For this challenging tour, pack essential cycling gear including a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, gloves, and sunglasses. Carry plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a first-aid kit. Sunscreen is crucial due to the island's strong sun. A fully charged phone with GPS capabilities is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Tour of the Island of Eivissa/Ibiza?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for this tour is around 6 hours and 13 minutes, based on an average pace. However, this does not include stops for rest, food, or sightseeing. Given its difficulty and length, many cyclists choose to complete it over a full day, allowing for breaks to enjoy the scenery and refuel.
